Q1.notes: Correct 4-group aggregation (returnflag × linestatus). All aggregate values match baseline exactly (within floating-point epsilon). Ordering correct (A|F, N|F, N|O, R|F). Total count_order = 59,142,609 rows processed. PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT: 20% faster than baseline (80ms vs 100ms).

Q3.notes: Top-10 results correctly ordered by revenue DESC (440715.22 → 415367.12). One minor floating-point difference in row 2: 439855.32 vs baseline 439855.33 (epsilon=0.01, acceptable). Customer filter selectivity 20.02% (300,276/1,500,000) matches baseline. Orders filtered to 1,461,923 matches baseline. All revenue values positive. PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT: 43% faster than baseline (310ms vs 540ms).

Q6.notes: Single aggregate value 1,230,113,636.01 matches baseline exactly. Result consistent with expected high selectivity (~0.7%) on 60M lineitem rows. PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT: 45% faster than baseline (60ms vs 110ms).

summary: All queries executed successfully at SF10 scale (59.9M lineitem, 15M orders, 1.5M customers). Compilation clean with no warnings. Ingestion skipped (reused existing persistent storage). Query execution time: 0.47 seconds total (Q1: 0.08s, Q3: 0.31s, Q6: 0.06s). All results semantically equivalent to baseline (iteration 1). PRIMARY METRIC IMPROVEMENT: Query execution time improved from 0.83s to 0.47s, representing a 43% overall speedup. Individual query improvements: Q1 20% faster, Q3 43% faster, Q6 45% faster. All results validated: correct row counts, positive values, proper ordering, semantically equivalent to baseline.
